hints.estate: 'estate' is not a Latin word — 'Estote' is the imperative of 'esse'
hints.sitis: 'sitis' would be subjunctive of 'esse' — 'Estote' is the imperative

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 2nd plural is 'estis' — 'Estote' is the imperative
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive 2nd plural would be 'sitis' — 'Estote' is the future imperative
hints.infinitive: Infinitive is 'esse' — 'Estote' is the imperative plural form

hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are principal parts of 'sto' (to stand)
hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are principal parts of 'edo' (to eat)
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are principal parts of 'eo' (to go)

hints.accusative: Accusative plural is also 'misericordes', but here it is a predicate nominative
hints.vocative: Vocative plural would also be 'misericordes', but this is a predicate adjective with 'estote'
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'misericordibus' — 'misericordes' is nominative

steps.prompt: What declension pattern does 'misericors, misericordis' follow?

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'misericordis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'misericordis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'misericordis' ends in -is
